title: AHP Conformance Discovery — Inventory Row
description: One conformance behavior emitted by a discovery angle (D1-D11). Sources never edit each other's rows; D11 reconciles by union of sources[] keyed on behavior-id. Every row MUST carry a real file:line citation in the fork.

behavior-id.description: Stable human-readable id: <domain>.<concept>.<scenario-class>.<discriminator>, 3-5 dot-separated segments of [A-Za-z0-9-]. Two angles emitting the same behavior-id mean the SAME behavior (D11 unions their sources).

source.description: Which discovery angle emitted this row.

method.description: The RPC method / notification / channel this behavior pertains to (e.g. 'initialize', 'session/subscribe'), or null when not method-scoped.

concept.description: The protocol concept: initialize, session-channel, reconnect-replay, StateAction:SessionTitleChanged, error:MethodNotFound, etc.

normative-level.description: RFC-2119 level when derived from spec prose; NONE for rows derived from schema/fixtures/code with no explicit normative keyword.

citation.description: MANDATORY real grounding. file:line must really exist in the fork and really contain excerpt.

file.description: Path relative to fork root, e.g. schema/commands.schema.json

line.description: 1-based line number, or null for whole-file / not-line-addressable citations.

excerpt.description: Verbatim snippet from file:line that grounds this behavior.

coverage.description: Coverage status. D1-D10 always emit 'unknown'; D11 reconciles to planned/covered/out-of-scope.

notes.description: Optional free-text: triage, cross-references, divergence detail, out-of-scope reason.

assertion.description: Optional: the spec-correct observable outcome this behavior asserts (what a conformance scenario would check).

params-shape-ref.description: Optional: pointer to the params/result schema shape, e.g. commands.schema.json#/$defs/InitializeParams.
